ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Are you able to get on and off the toilet?
Notes: 1=Unable to do; 2=With much difficulty; 3=With some difficulty; 4=With a little difficulty; 5=Without any difficulty

ElementDescription: Are you able to step up and down curbs?
Notes: 1=Unable to do; 2=With much difficulty; 3=With some difficulty; 4=With a little difficulty; 5=Without any difficulty

ElementDescription: Are you able to get in and out of a car?
Notes: 1=Unable to do; 2=With much difficulty; 3=With some difficulty; 4=With a little difficulty; 5=Without any difficulty

Notes: 1=Unable to do; 2=With much difficulty; 3=With some difficulty; 4=With a little difficulty; 5=Without any difficulty

Notes: 1=Unable to do; 2=With much difficulty; 3=With some difficulty; 4=With a little difficulty; 5=Without any difficulty

ElementDescription: How much DIFFICULTY do you currently have standing up from an armless straight chair (e.g., dining room chair)?
Notes: 1=Can't do; 2=A lot of difficulty; 3=Some difficulty; 4=A little difficulty; 5=No difficulty

ElementDescription: How much DIFFICULTY do you currently have sitting down on and standing up from a chair with arms?
Notes: 1=Can't do; 2=A lot of difficulty; 3=Some difficulty; 4=A little difficulty; 5=No difficulty

ElementDescription: How much DIFFICULTY do you currently have moving from sitting at the side of the bed to lying down on your back?
Notes: 1=Can't do; 2=A lot of difficulty; 3=Some difficulty; 4=A little difficulty; 5=No difficulty

ElementDescription: How much DIFFICULTY do you currently have standing up from a low, soft couch?
Notes: 1=Can't do; 2=A lot of difficulty; 3=Some difficulty; 4=A little difficulty; 5=No difficulty

ElementDescription: How much DIFFICULTY do you currently have going up and down a flight of stairs inside, using a handrail?
Notes: 1=Can't do; 2=A lot of difficulty; 3=Some difficulty; 4=A little difficulty; 5=No difficulty

ElementDescription: How much DIFFICULTY do you currently have walking on uneven surfaces (e.g., grass, dirt road or sidewalk)?
Notes: 1=Can't do; 2=A lot of difficulty; 3=Some difficulty; 4=A little difficulty; 5=No difficulty

ElementDescription: How much DIFFICULTY do you currently have walking around one floor of your home?
Notes: 1=Can't do; 2=A lot of difficulty; 3=Some difficulty; 4=A little difficulty; 5=No difficulty

ElementDescription: How much DIFFICULTY do you currently have taking a 20-minute brisk walk, without stopping to rest?
Notes: 1=Can't do; 2=A lot of difficulty; 3=Some difficulty; 4=A little difficulty; 5=No difficulty

ElementDescription: How much DIFFICULTY do you currently have walking on a slippery surface, outdoors?
Notes: 1=Can't do; 2=A lot of difficulty; 3=Some difficulty; 4=A little difficulty; 5=No difficulty

ElementDescription: How much DIFFICULTY do you currently have climbing stairs step over step without a handrail? (alternating feet)?

ElementDescription: How much DIFFICULTY do you currently have walking in a dark room without falling?
Notes: 1=Can't do; 2=A lot of difficulty; 3=Some difficulty; 4=A little difficulty; 5=No difficulty
